## 🚀 New Feature: [Feature Name]

### 📝 Description
Provide a clear description of what this feature does and the problem it solves.

### 🎯 Key Changes
- Added `X` component to handle `Y`.
- Implemented new API endpoint `/api/v1/feature`.
- Integrated `Z` library for data processing.

| Desktop / Main View | Mobile / Alternate View |

> [!TIP]

### 🧪 Quality Assurance
- [ ] **Unit Tests:** New logic is covered by tests.
- [ ] **Integration Tests:** Feature works with existing systems.
- [ ] **Manual Testing:** Verified on [Browser/Device].

### 📚 Documentation
- [ ] Updated README or internal docs.
- [ ] Inline code comments added for complex logic.

## 🛠️ Refactor: [Module/Area Name]

### 💡 Motivation
Why are we refactoring this now? (e.g., Reducing tech debt, improving readability, fixing a "code smell").

### 🔄 Changes
**Before:**
- Describe the old structure/logic.

**After:**
- Describe the new, improved structure/logic.

### 📝 Additional Notes & Context
Add any extra context, caveats, or observations here.

* **Out of scope:** (e.g., "I noticed `LegacyAuth.js` needs a similar refactor, but I kept it out of this PR to keep the review manageable. See Issue #123.")
* **References:** [Link to relevant Slack thread, Jira ticket, or design doc]

## 🎨 UI Change Description

| Before | After |
| :--- | :--- |

## 🛠️ Technical Details
- **Design Reference:** [Link to Figma/Adobe XD]
- **Components Modified:** `Button`, `LoginForm`


## ♿ Accessibility (A11y)
- [ ] Contrast ratios meet WCAG AA standards.
- [ ] Interactive elements are keyboard accessible (Tab/Enter).
- [ ] Screen reader labels (`aria-label`) are provided where necessary.

## 🧪 How to Test
1. Go to the `/login` page.
2. Check if the button color changes on hover.
3. Verify the layout doesn't break on mobile screens.
